calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-15-2016 Vacated

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2016 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-19-2016 Continued To Sep-12-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar May-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alexander Volchegursky of Law Offices of Alexander Volchegursky telephone number 415-986-4000 for Plaintiff Speck Cab Co.
Heidi Machen of Machen Law telephone number 415-626-1140 for Defendant Town Taxi Inc.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FOG CITY CAB INC'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings as to all three claims alleged in the second amended complaint is granted without leave to amend as to the claim for negligent interference with prospective economic advantage and denied as to the section 17200 and intentional interference with prospective economic advantage claims. Defendant's abstention argument is denied in the court's discretion. Even if it would have made sense to abstain to permit determination of the issues raised by plaintiff SF Town Taxi at the outset of the case, to do so now, a month before trial, as this case has been pending in this court for a year and half is unfairly prejudicial to the plaintiff. Plaintiff's opposition memorandum fails to address defendant's argument that the negligent interference claim fails because defendant does not owe a duty of care to plaintiff. The argument is correct and requires adjudication of the negligent interference claim in favor of defendant. The section 17200 claim is adequately alleged and any greater specificity that defendant needed about that claim could have been addressed by discovery. Plaintiff has sufficiently alleged independently wrongful conduct in the form of violation of municipal regulations to support its intentional interference claim. Mr. Volchegursky is to prepare a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
